description: Centralized generator of human-readable sequential identifiers for any entity that needs them — purchase order numbers, invoice numbers, contract numbers, NF sequences. Guarantees uniqueness within a scope (company, branch, or global) without gaps under normal operation. Modules never implement their own sequence logic.
consumers: any module that needs sequential document numbers — called once per entity creation, before insert

SequenceKey.constraints: unique identifier for this sequence. Format: '{module}.{entityType}.{scope}', e.g. 'purchasing.PurchaseOrder.company:uuid', 'financial.Invoice.branch:uuid', 'mdm.NF.company:uuid'. Indexed for lookup

Prefix.constraints: optional static prefix prepended to the formatted number, e.g. 'PO-', 'NF-', 'CTR-'. Max 10 chars

CurrentValue.constraints: last issued number. Incremented atomically by the routine using SELECT FOR UPDATE. Never written directly

Increment.constraints: step between values, almost always 1. Values other than 1 require explicit justification in the module ADR

Padding.constraints: zero-pad the numeric part to this length, e.g. padding 6 produces '000042'. Default 0 means no padding

YearSegment.constraints: if true, the year is included in the formatted output and CurrentValue resets to 1 on January 1st. e.g. 'PO-2025-000001'. Required for NF-e sequences in Brazil

ScopeType.constraints: 'company' is the most common — one sequence per company per entity type

ScopeId.constraints: the companyId or branchId when ScopeType is 'company' or 'branch'. Null when ScopeType is 'global'

LastIssuedAt.constraints: timestamp of the last next() call — used for monitoring and year-reset detection

CreatedAt.constraints: when this sequence was first registered

Details.constraints: optional metadata — e.g. legal series identifier for NF-e, reset history log. Stored in DynamoDB only

postgresql.note: primary lookup — must be unique and fast
list all sequences for a company or branch

dynamodb.note: backup of full sequence record including Details — used to restore local table if needed

consistencyContract.note: CurrentValue must be sync — a gap or duplicate in sequence is a business error. Details is informational and can lag

retention.postgresql: permanent — sequence state must never be deleted while the module is active

persistence.note: CurrentValue is the only mutable field — updated atomically via SELECT FOR UPDATE on every next() call

formattedOutput.description: The next() routine returns a formatted string, not a raw integer
